verses.text: God bids Hosea take 'a wife of whoredoms,' for the land has played the harlot against the LORD. A living parable. The prophet's marriage becomes a picture of God's grief over an unfaithful people.
God bids Hosea take 'a wife of whoredoms,' for the land has played the harlot against the LORD. A living parable. The prophet's marriage becomes a picture of God's grief over an unfaithful people.
God bids Hosea take 'a wife of whoredoms,' for the land has played the harlot against the LORD. A living parable. The prophet's marriage becomes a picture of God's grief over an unfaithful people.
Gomer bears a son named Jezreel, for God will avenge bloodshed and end the kingdom of Israel. A name of judgment. Even the children's names preach the coming reckoning.
Gomer bears a son named Jezreel, for God will avenge bloodshed and end the kingdom of Israel. A name of judgment. Even the children's names preach the coming reckoning.
A daughter, Lo-ruhamah, 'no mercy,' for God will no longer pity Israel, though He will save Judah. Withdrawn mercy. The withholding of pity is itself a fearful judgment.
A daughter, Lo-ruhamah, 'no mercy,' for God will no longer pity Israel, though He will save Judah. Withdrawn mercy. The withholding of pity is itself a fearful judgment.
A son, Lo-ammi, 'not my people,' for God says, 'ye are not my people, and I will not be your God.' Covenant broken. The sweetest bond is severed by persistent sin.
A son, Lo-ammi, 'not my people,' for God says, 'ye are not my people, and I will not be your God.' Covenant broken. The sweetest bond is severed by persistent sin.

Yet Israel shall be as the sand, and 'where it was said... Ye are not my people... they shall be called the sons of the living God.' Grace beyond rejection. Paul sees here the calling of the Gentiles.
